Ensure "Disable Guest Account" is set to "True"
recommendations.description: The Guest account allows users to log in to the computer without requiring user authentication. When enabled, the Guest account provides access to the system with a restricted set of privileges but does not require a password. The account is recreated every time a guest logs out.
recommendations.rationale: Disabling the Guest account prevents unauthorized access to the system and its resources. A Guest account, even with limited privileges, could be exploited to: • Access shared network resources • View non-protected files and folders • Launch attacks against the system or network • Gather information about the organization's computing environment • Create a persistent presence on the device
recommendations.impact: Disabling the Guest account will prevent users from logging in without authentication credentials. Users who previously relied on Guest access will need valid credentials to access the system. This may affect shared workstations where Guest access was intentionally enabled for temporary use. App Store

recommendations.title: Ensure "Restrict Store Software Update Only" is set to "True"
recommendations.description: This setting prevents users from accessing the App Store application on managed macOS devices, while still allowing system software updates to be installed through the Software Update mechanism.
recommendations.rationale: Restricting access to the App Store while maintaining software update capabilities helps organizations maintain control over their software environment. The setting supports a controlled application deployment strategy where software distribution is managed through organizational channels rather than individual user installations.
recommendations.impact: Users will be unable to launch the App Store and install applications through it. Organizations must ensure they have alternative methods for software deployment, such as through MDM or approved software distribution channels.

Full Disk Encryption securely. 2.3.1 FIleVault

recommendations.title: Ensure "User Enters Missing Info" is set to "True"
recommendations.description: This policy setting ensures that users are prompted to enter any missing user account information during FileVault encryption setup. When enabled, the system will require users to confirm their login credentials if they haven't been properly configured for FileVault access.
recommendations.rationale: Enforcing complete user account configuration ensures that FileVault encryption is properly linked to authorized user credentials. This maintains secure access to encrypted data through the user's standard login password.
recommendations.impact: Users may be prompted to verify their login credentials during initial FileVault setup if the system detects missing or incomplete account information.

recommendations.title: Ensure "Enable" is set to "On"
recommendations.description: FileVault provides full disk encryption (XTS-AES-128 with 256-bit key) for the entire system volume on macOS devices. This policy setting ensures FileVault encryption is enabled and enforced.
recommendations.rationale: FileVault encryption protects data at rest from unauthorized access, particularly in cases of lost or stolen devices. Without full disk encryption, an attacker with physical access to the device could access data via alternative boot methods or by removing the drive. FileVault ensures that data remains protected behind strong encryption that requires valid credentials to decrypt.
recommendations.impact: Enabling FileVault requires an initial encryption process that generally takes seconds on newer devices, and can take hours on older hardware with large disk drives. Minor system performance impact may be observed, though modern processors with hardware encryption support minimize this effect.

recommendations.title: Ensure "Defer Force At User Login Max Bypass Attempts" is set to "0"
recommendations.description: This policy setting determines the maximum number of times a user can bypass the FileVault login prompt at startup before encryption is forcefully enabled. Setting this value to "0" ensures users cannot defer or bypass the FileVault encryption requirement.
recommendations.rationale: Allowing users to bypass FileVault encryption leaves organizational data vulnerable to unauthorized access in case of device theft or loss. Setting the maximum bypass attempts to "0" enforces immediate FileVault encryption, ensuring that all system data is protected with strong encryption from the moment the policy is applied, at the user's next login.
recommendations.impact: Users will be required to enable FileVault encryption immediately without the option to defer. This may cause a brief delay during the initial encryption process, and users will need to remember their device password. If users forget their credentials, they will need to use the organizational recovery key to regain access to their data. Page 32 Internal Only - General From the Microsoft Intune admin center: 1. recommendations.title: Ensure "Show Recovery Key" is set to "Disabled"
recommendations.description: Users will not be able to view their recovery key through the System Settings interface or during FileVault initialization.
recommendations.rationale: Displaying FileVault recovery keys to users increases the risk of keys being compromised through unauthorized documentation, sharing, or capture. By preventing the display of recovery keys, organizations maintain better control over recovery procedures and ensure that recovery disk decryption can only be performed through approved organizational channels.
recommendations.impact: All recovery operations will need to be handled through organizational IT support channels. Organizations must ensure they have proper key escrow systems in place and documented procedures for handling disk recovery scenarios before implementing this policy.

recommendations.title: Ensure "Force Enable In Setup Assistant" is set to "True"
recommendations.description: When enabled, the system will prompt for FileVault enablement during initial device setup immediately after MDM enrollment, ensuring early-stage disk encryption implementation. This will only apply to devices that are MDM enrolled before the device has been set up (or after a factory reset).
recommendations.rationale: Forcing FileVault enablement during Setup Assistant ensures that devices are encrypted from their initial deployment, eliminating the scenario of sensitive data being written to an unencrypted disk before encryption activation. This also reduces the likelihood of user postponement or avoidance of encryption implementation.
recommendations.impact: Devices will require slightly more time during initial setup to configure FileVault encryption.

Login 2.4.1 Login Window Behavior

recommendations.title: Ensure "Show Full Name" is set to "True"
recommendations.description: This policy setting controls whether the macOS login window displays a name and password dialog instead of a list of users. When enabled, users must enter both their username and password to authenticate.
recommendations.rationale: Displaying a list of users in the login window provides potential attackers with partial authentication credentials, as it reveals valid usernames on the system. By requiring users to enter both their username and password, the organization reduces the risk of unauthorized access and makes passcode cracking more difficult since attackers must guess both components of the login credentials.
recommendations.impact: Users will need to type both their username and password when logging in, rather than selecting their account from a list. This may slightly increase the time needed to log in but provides enhanced security. Users will need to remember their exact username in addition to their password.

recommendations.title: Ensure "Disable Login Items Suppression" is set to "True"
recommendations.description: This setting prevents users from disabling the automatic launch of login items by holding down the Shift key during login. When set to True, all designated login items will launch regardless of user intervention.
recommendations.rationale: Organizations often deploy critical security software that must run at system startup to maintain security posture. Allowing users to suppress these login items could create security gaps by preventing security applications from launching, potentially leaving the system insecure. Page 51 Internal Only - General

Managed Settings 2.5.1 MDM Options

recommendations.title: Ensure "Activation Lock Allowed While Supervised" is set appropriately
recommendations.description: When Activation Lock is enabled, a device cannot be erased or reactivated without the associated Apple ID credentials, including after a factory reset. This setting does not enable Activation Lock directly, but instead controls if users are permitted to enable this feature through Find My.
recommendations.rationale: When set to Allowed: Activation Lock provides an additional security layer beyond device passcodes and FileVault by preventing device reactivation after factory reset. While passcodes and FileVault protect data, Activation Lock specifically deters device theft by making the hardware itself unusable to thieves. When set to Blocked: Ensures devices can be easily repurposed when users leave the organization. When passcode and FileVault are already providing data protection, Activation Lock may create unnecessary administrative overhead without significant additional security benefits for organization-managed devices.
recommendations.impact: When set to Allowed: May complicate device management if users leave without removing their Apple ID. Requires appropriate offboarding procedures or Apple Business Manager. When set to Blocked: Removes a theft deterrent from devices. Organizations should implement alternative security measures to compensate.

Networking 2.6.1 Content Caching

recommendations.title: Ensure "Auto Activation" is set to "False"
recommendations.description: Content caching stores and serves various types of content from Apple, including software updates, app store purchases, iCloud data, and other downloadable content to reduce internet bandwidth usage. When "Auto Activation" is enabled, content caching automatically activates when possible and prevents manual deactivation. This capability requires macOS clients to function as P2P nodes for Apple content distribution.
recommendations.rationale: P2P sharing services should not be enabled on endpoints using untrusted networks. Disabling content caching's auto activation reduces the attack surface by preventing automatic network service initiation. Content caching creates additional network connections and locally stored content, which may introduce security risks in managed environments.
recommendations.impact: Disabling auto activation will increase bandwidth usage between local subnets and the Internet as devices download updates, apps, and iCloud content directly from Apple's servers rather than local caches. Organizations should ensure adequate bandwidth is available for all Apple content and services. Page 60 Internal Only - General

recommendations.title: Ensure "Enable Stealth Mode" is set to "True"
recommendations.description: Stealth Mode prevents the system from responding to probing requests such as ICMP ping, effectively making the system appear invisible to network scanning.
recommendations.rationale: Enabling stealth mode helps prevent malicious actors from discovering the system through network scanning techniques. By not responding to probing attempts, the system becomes more difficult to detect and target, reducing the attack surface and reconnaissance activities.
recommendations.impact: When stealth mode is enabled, legitimate network diagnostic tools that rely on ICMP ping responses may fail to detect or communicate with the system. Other network tools that measure activity and approved applications will work as expected.

recommendations.title: Ensure "Enable Firewall" is set to "True"
recommendations.description: The macOS firewall protects systems from unwanted network connections by blocking unauthorized incoming connections. When enabled, the firewall analyzes inbound traffic and blocks connections that don't meet the rules defined in the firewall configuration.
recommendations.rationale: When enabled, the Firewall blocks incoming connections that haven't been authorized, reducing the attack surface from network-based threats. This is particularly important for devices that connect to untrusted networks.
recommendations.impact: Enabling the firewall may impact legitimate applications that require incoming connections if they are not properly configured in the firewall settings. Users might experience issues with file sharing, screen sharing, or other network services until appropriate rules are established.

Profile Removal Password

recommendations.title: Ensure "Removal Password" is absent or is securely configured with restricted access
recommendations.description: The profile removal password setting in Intune device management controls whether a local user can remove the management profile from a macOS device. When this setting is not configured (absent) or is configured with restricted access, it prevents the unauthorized removal of management profiles that enforce organizational security policies.
recommendations.rationale: If a profile removal password is set and becomes widely known, users could remove management profiles and circumvent organizational security policies. Either maintaining no on-device profile removal password (relying on Intune's remote unenrollment controls) or implementing a securely configured password with restricted access ensures that only authorized administrators can remove profiles when necessary.

Restrictions

recommendations.title: Ensure "Allow Bluetooth Sharing Modification" is set to "False"
recommendations.description: Bluetooth Sharing allows users to exchange files with other Bluetooth-enabled devices. Setting "Allow Bluetooth Sharing Modification" to "False" prevents users from changing the Bluetooth sharing settings in System Settings.
recommendations.rationale: Controlling Bluetooth Sharing capability at an organizational level prevents unauthorized data transfers between organization-managed devices and external Bluetooth-enabled devices.
recommendations.impact: If Bluetooth Sharing was disabled before policy application (system default), it will remain disabled. If Bluetooth Sharing was enabled by the user before policy application, it will remain enabled and cannot be modified by the user. A separate script should be deployed to ensure Bluetooth Sharing is actually disabled on all systems, as this policy only restricts user control of the setting.

recommendations.title: Ensure "Allow Password Sharing" is set to "False"
recommendations.description: The Password Sharing feature allows users to share passwords with nearby devices using AirDrop. When enabled, macOS users can share saved passwords from their keychain with other Apple devices in proximity.
recommendations.rationale: Disabling password sharing via AirDrop prevents potential exposure of organizational credentials. If this feature is enabled, users could intentionally or unintentionally share sensitive passwords with unauthorized recipients.
recommendations.impact: Users will need to use organization-approved methods for sharing credentials when required.

recommendations.title: Ensure "Allow File Sharing Modification" is set to "False"
recommendations.description: File sharing allows users to share folders and files from their Mac with other users over a network using SMB and other file sharing protocols. The "Allow File Sharing Modification" setting controls whether users can modify the file sharing configuration through System Settings. When this setting is disabled, users cannot enable or disable file sharing services or modify shared folder permissions.
recommendations.rationale: Preventing users from modifying file sharing settings helps organizations maintain control over network resource sharing. Unrestricted file sharing can lead to unauthorized data exposure, creates additional network entry points, and may allow sensitive data to be transferred outside organizational boundaries.
recommendations.impact: If file sharing was disabled before policy application (system default), it will remain disabled. If file sharing was enabled by the user before policy application, it will remain enabled and cannot be modified by the user. A separate script should be deployed to ensure file sharing is actually disabled on all systems, as this policy only restricts user control of the setting. Page 79 Internal Only - General

recommendations.title: Ensure "Allow Air Play Incoming Requests" is set to "False"
recommendations.description: AirPlay is an Apple technology that allows wireless streaming of audio, video, and screen sharing between devices. When enabled on macOS, it allows the Mac to receive AirPlay requests and function as an AirPlay receiver, permitting other Apple devices to stream content to the Mac's display.
recommendations.rationale: Disabling AirPlay incoming requests prevents unauthorized devices from establishing AirPlay connections to organizational Macs. The feature broadcasts device presence and processes incoming connection requests, which could be leveraged for network reconnaissance or denial-of-service attacks. Although AirPlay connections require user approval on the receiving Mac, persistent connection requests may disrupt workflow, while unauthorized screen sharing could lead to exposure of sensitive information.
recommendations.impact: Users will not be able to use their Mac as an AirPlay receiver for other Apple devices.

recommendations.title: Ensure "Allow Cloud Photo Library" is set to "False"
recommendations.description: iCloud Photos (formerly known as iCloud Photo Library) is a cloud-based service that automatically stores and synchronizes photos and videos across Apple devices using Apple's iCloud infrastructure. When enabled, all photos and videos taken on the device are uploaded to iCloud storage and made available from other devices signed in with the same iCloud account.
recommendations.rationale: Disabling iCloud Photo Library prevents the synchronization of organizational photos and videos to Apple's iCloud services. This limitation helps maintain data residency requirements and ensures sensitive visual content remains within approved storage locations.
recommendations.impact: Users will not be able to sync or access their photos and videos through iCloud Photos. Local device photo storage and management will continue to function normally through the Photos app.

recommendations.title: Ensure "Allow Cloud Document Sync" is set to "False"
recommendations.description: iCloud Document Sync is an app-level cloud-based service that enables applications to store and synchronize documents and data through iCloud. When enabled, this feature allows apps to automatically save files to iCloud storage, making these documents accessible across multiple devices using the same Apple ID.
recommendations.rationale: Disabling iCloud Document Sync prevents applications from synchronizing organizational documents and app data to Apple's iCloud services. This limitation helps maintain data residency requirements and ensures sensitive information remains within approved storage locations that are under organizational control.
recommendations.impact: Applications will not be able to automatically save or sync documents with iCloud. Apps that utilize iCloud document storage capabilities will be limited to storing data locally on the device or on organization-approved storage solutions.

recommendations.title: Ensure "Enforced Software Update Delay" is absent or less than or equal to 30 days
recommendations.description: The Enforced Software Update Delay setting controls the number of days before users are notified of available software updates on managed macOS devices. When configured, this setting prevents pending updates from appearing to users until the specified delay period has elapsed from the update's release date. The setting affects both system and app software update notifications. The optimal configuration is to leave this setting not configured (absent), allowing users to be notified of updates as soon as they become available.
recommendations.rationale: Apple software updates almost always include security updates. By delaying update notifications, organizations increase the time systems remain vulnerable to security issues that have been patched in the available updates.
recommendations.impact: Organizations may use a short delay period to verify compatibility with specialized software before notifications reach end users, but should minimize this delay to maintain security. Organizations should balance their testing needs against security risks associated with delayed notifications. Page 93 Internal Only - General

recommendations.title: Ensure "Allow Activity Continuation" is set to "False"
recommendations.description: Activity Continuation, also known as Handoff, allows users to start work on one macOS device and continue on another Apple device. This feature requires devices to be signed into the same Apple ID and uses Bluetooth and WiFi connections for nearby devices, with iCloud handling transfers between distant devices.
recommendations.rationale: Activity Continuation transfers application state and data across devices without maintaining managed application boundaries. This could lead to organizational data being transferred devices outside the organization's control.
recommendations.impact: Users will be unable to use Handoff functionality between their managed macOS devices and other Apple devices.

recommendations.title: Ensure "Allow Cloud Bookmarks" is set to "False"
recommendations.description: iCloud Bookmarks is a feature that synchronizes web browser bookmarks and reading list items across Apple devices using Apple's iCloud infrastructure. When enabled, bookmarks created in Safari and other compatible browsers are automatically uploaded to iCloud and made available on all devices signed into the same Apple ID.
recommendations.rationale: Disabling iCloud Bookmarks prevents the synchronization of browser bookmarks to Apple's iCloud services. When combined with the "Ask For Password" policy defined in the system configuration section of the benchmark, this will enforce a device lock after 8 minutes of inactivity.
recommendations.rationale: Extended periods of inactivity on an unlocked device present security risks. An unattended device may permit unauthorized users to access sensitive data or perform malicious actions. Limiting inactivity to 8 minutes or less reduces the window of opportunity for unauthorized access. This control requires the "Ask For Password" policy to ensure screen saver activation triggers device locking. While automatic locking provides a security fail-safe, users should be trained to manually lock devices when leaving them unattended and should be familiar with keyboard shortcuts for device locking.

recommendations.title: Ensure "Min Complex Characters" is set to "1" or more
recommendations.description: This setting enforces at least one complex character to be contained within user device passcodes. Complex characters are non-alphanumeric symbols such as: &, %, $, #, !, @, and other special characters. A space character is considered a complex character, therefore facilitating the use of passphases without symbols.
recommendations.rationale: Complex characters make passcodes more resistant to password cracking attempts.
recommendations.impact: If this policy is applied to current users, they may need to create new passwords on next login if their existing password does not meet this requirement. When setting a new password, if the complex character requirement is not met, users will see that the Contain at last one non-alphanumeric character condition is not met when prompted to enter a new passcode. recommendations.title: Ensure "PIN History" is set to "15" or more
recommendations.description: The PIN history setting enforces uniqueness requirements by maintaining a record of previously used passcodes. When set to 15, users must create a passcode that differs from their last 15 used passcodes.
recommendations.rationale: When users can cycle through a few favorite passcodes, the security benefits of periodic passcode changes are undermined.
recommendations.impact: When setting a new password, if the passcode history uniqueness requirement is not met, users will see that the Not be the same as the previous 15 passwords condition is not met when prompted to enter a new passcode.

recommendations.title: Ensure "Force PIN" is set to "True"
recommendations.description: This setting requires users to establish a device passcode (PIN) for device authentication. When enabled, users must create and use a passcode to unlock their device.
recommendations.rationale: Without a passcode requirement, a device may remain accessible to anyone who gains physical possession of it.
recommendations.impact: If this policy is applied to current users, they may need to create a passcode on next login if they do not currently have a passcode set.

recommendations.title: Ensure "Password Content Regex" is defined
recommendations.description: The "Password Content Regex" setting enforces password complexity requirements by defining a regular expression pattern with which passwords must comply. When configured, the system evaluates new passwords against this pattern, rejecting those that fail to match. This setting utilizes ICU syntax for pattern matching and can be used to prevent passwords from containing specific words or patterns. The provided regex pattern (?i)^(?!.*(password|cis|example)).* blocks passwords containing defined terms regardless of case sensitivity.
recommendations.rationale: Passwords that contain terms like company names, product names, or common industry words are significantly easier to guess through dictionary attacks and social engineering tactics. Implementing a content regex pattern prevents users from selecting passwords that include defined terms, thereby strengthening the organization's overall password security posture.
recommendations.impact: If this policy is applied to current users, they may need to create new passwords on next login if their existing passwords contain blocked terms. When setting a new password, if the regex requirement is not met, users will see that ProfilePayload:<ID>:customRegex condition is not met when prompted to enter a new passcode. This may cause some users to wonder why their password does not meet password requirements, therefore help desk staff may need to provide guidance to users on acceptable password patterns. recommendations.title: Ensure "Max Failed Attempts" is set to "5" or less
recommendations.description: This setting defines the maximum number of failed attempts allowed when entering a passcode at the device's lock screen. When used in combination with the "Minutes Until Failed Login Reset" policy when the attempt threshold is reached, a time delay is imposed before another passcode attempt can be made.
recommendations.rationale: Implementing a passcode attempt restriction mechanism protects against automated and manual password guessing attacks.
recommendations.impact: Users who legitimately forget their passcode or make repeated typing errors may experience temporary device access delays.

recommendations.title: Ensure "Allow Simple Passcode" is set to "False"
recommendations.description: This setting prevents the use of simple device authentication passcodes on macOS devices by disallowing passcodes that contain two consecutive identical characters or three sequential characters.
recommendations.rationale: Disabling simple passcodes eliminates easily predictable patterns by preventing consecutive repeating characters (such as "11") and sequential patterns (such as "123" or "abc"). This significantly increases the complexity of passcodes and enhances the security of the device against password guessing and brute force attempts.
recommendations.impact: Users will be unable to use passcodes containing any two consecutive identical characters (such as "rr" in "corridor") or three sequential characters (such as "abc" or "123"). This restriction applies to all parts of the passcode, which means many common words and conventional password patterns will be disallowed. If this policy is applied to current users, they may need to create new passwords on next login if their existing passcode does not meet this requirement. When setting a new password, if the no simple passcode requirement is not met, users will see that the ``Not have two consecutive, or three sequential characters``` condition is not met when prompted to enter a new passcode. recommendations.title: Ensure "Minutes Until Failed Login Reset" is set to "15" or more
recommendations.description: This setting determines the number of minutes that must elapse before the failed login counter is reset after reaching the maximum number of unsuccessful login attempts. This policy requires that the "Max Failed Attempts" policy is also configured, and it starts a time-based delay before additional login attempts can be processed.
recommendations.rationale: Implementing a lockout duration provides an essential control against automated and manual password guessing attempts.
recommendations.impact: Users who exceed the maximum failed login attempts must wait for the specified duration before attempting to authenticate again, even with valid credentials. During this waiting period, users may see the message Your account has been disabled. Contact your system administrator for more information. After the time period this message can be ignored and another attempt it allowed. Page 189 Internal Only - General

recommendations.description: This setting requires that device passcodes contain at least one alphabetic character (a- z, A-Z) in addition to numerals and other required character types.
recommendations.rationale: Alphanumeric passcodes significantly increase the possible character combinations compared to numeric-only passcodes.
recommendations.impact: If this policy is applied to current users, they may need to create new passwords on next login if their existing passcode does not meet this requirement. When setting a new password, if the alphanumeric requirement is not met, users will see that the Contain at least one number and one alphabetic character condition is not met when prompted to enter a new passcode.

recommendations.title: Ensure "Min Length" is set to "15" or more
recommendations.description: When configured to "15" or more, users must create passcodes that contain at least 15 characters when setting or changing their device passcode.
recommendations.rationale: Shorter passcodes are more susceptible to brute force attacks and dictionary-based password cracking attempts. Enforcing a minimum 15-character passcode requirement exponentially increases the number of possible passcodes, making automated cracking methods significantly more difficult.
recommendations.impact: Users may need to adapt to using longer passphrases. This presents an opportunity to encourage the use of non-common passphrases that are easier to remember but harder to guess. If this policy is applied to current users, they may need to create new passwords on next login if their existing passcode does not meet this requirement. When setting a new password, if the minimum length requirement is not met, users will see that the Contain at least 15 characters condition is not met when prompted to enter a new passcode. recommendations.title: Ensure "Display Sleep Timer" is set to "10" or less, but not 0
recommendations.description: The "Display Sleep Timer" controls the duration of inactivity before the display enters sleep mode. This setting is required for reliable System Sleep Timer functionality. Display sleep only turns off the screen and does not trigger system re-authentication.
recommendations.rationale: Setting an appropriate display sleep timer ensures the System Sleep Timer functions reliably, which is critical for FileVault security.
recommendations.impact: Users will need to reactivate displays after inactivity periods. Additional screen saver passcode controls defined in this benchmark will enforce passcode re-entry when implemented. Page 211 Internal Only - General

recommendations.description: The "System Sleep Timer" controls the duration of inactivity before system sleep activation. This operates independently from the display sleep timer but requires "Display Sleep Timer" configuration to function consistently.
recommendations.rationale: This setting ensures unattended devices enter sleep mode promptly. When the "Destroy FV Key On Standby" recommendation is applied, this will ensure FileVault encryption keys are effectively destroyed on sleep, requiring re-authentication to access encrypted data.

recommendations.title: Ensure "Wake on LAN" is set to "False"
recommendations.description: This setting controls whether a Mac can be awakened from sleep through network access. When enabled, the system can be remotely activated by wake packets sent over the network, allowing the computer to be accessed when it would otherwise be in sleep mode.
recommendations.rationale: Disabling Wake on LAN prevents unauthorized users from remotely waking the system, which could be used in an attempt to gain access to an secured or unattended device.
recommendations.impact: In modern computing environments, disabling Wake on LAN has minimal operational impact. Modern MDM solutions like Intune do not rely on Wake on LAN functionality for management operations, instead communicating with devices when they are online and applying policies when devices check in. recommendations.title: Ensure "Destroy FV Key On Standby" is set to "True"
recommendations.description: When enabled, the FileVault keys are completely destroyed from the SMC or RAM when the system enters sleep, requiring the user to re-enter their password upon wakeup to decrypt the disk.
recommendations.rationale: FileVault keys temporarily stored in SMC or RAM during standby could be vulnerable to extraction through cold boot attacks or memory analysis. By destroying these keys during standby, an additional layer of protection is provided for encrypted data, even if an attacker gains physical access to the device while in standby mode.
recommendations.impact: This may slightly increase the time required for a system to wake from standby, as the user must re-authenticate to decrypt the FileVault volume.

recommendations.description: When enabled, the system will prompt for authentication whenever the screen saver is deactivated. When combined with the "Max Inactivity" policy defined in the passcode subsection of the benchmark, this will enforce a device lock when the screen saver is triggered.
recommendations.rationale: Requiring password authentication after screen saver activation ensures that only authorized users can regain access to the system. recommendations.title: Ensure "Allow User Overrides" is set to "False"
recommendations.description: This setting controls whether users can approve system extensions that aren't explicitly allowed by configuration profiles. System extensions are software components that extend the functionality of macOS by integrating with the kernel or other system services without requiring kernel extensions (kexts).
recommendations.rationale: System extensions can access sensitive system resources and function. Examples include network content filters that can intercept traffic, endpoint security tools that can monitor system events, and DNS proxies that can redirect queries. When users can approve such extensions, they may authorize potentially malicious components that compromise security or violate organizational policies. This restriction ensures only administrator-vetted system extensions run on managed devices.
recommendations.impact: Users will be unable to approve system extensions. Legitimate system extensions that are needed but not pre-approved via configuration profiles will be blocked until administrators explicitly allow them. Some previously approved third-party system extensions may cease to function after this policy is applied. To allow specific system extensions to operate, administrators must add entries to the "Allowed Team Identifiers" list within the "System Extensions" configuration. Team IDs can be discovered on devices using the Terminal command systemextensionsctl list, or found within the application's Info.plist file. The Team ID remains consistent across all devices and through software updates, enabling efficient management of approved extensions. Once added to the allowed list, the application will function normally, requesting necessary extension permissions that can be granted. Page 229 Internal Only - General
